content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 11, 1989

Robert Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-495

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

You have requested advice on behalf of CAL-HY-PAC and its
current treasurer Lance H. Olson regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act of 1974.¹

FACTS

1. CAL-HY-PAC is a sponsored committee of the California
Dental Hygienists' Association.

2. CAL-HY-PAC is funded through dues allocations from dues
paid to the California Dental Hygienists' Association by its
members. The dues are paid annually to the national association,
which then forwards the California association's pro-rata share of
the dues to the California association. Of that pro-rata share, a
certain amount is earmarked for contributions to CAL-HY-PAC. In
past years, the amount has been either $10 or $5 per member per
year.

3. In addition to the dues allocation, CAL-HY-PAC has its
own fundraising mechanism. However, few members of the
association ever contribute $100 or more per year to CAL-HY-PAC.

4. Although the contributions are allocated in the dues
payments made by the members each annual period, because of
vagaries in the system, CAL-HY-PAC receives these payments over
several months' time. Thus, in some instances, there has been
more than a year delay in transmitting the dues allocations from
the national association to the California association to its
sponsored committee.

5. As treasurer for CAL-HY-PAC, your office recently
received two checks which exceeded $2,500 from the California
association. These checks represent dues allocations from July
1987 to September 1988.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Robert Leidigh
Page Two

     6.  CAL-HY-PAC has disclosed the contributions on its
campaign reports for the periods in which the funds were actually
received by CAL-HY-PAC.

QUESTIONS

     1.  May checks recently received by CAL-HY-PAC be treated as
contributions from the members of the association?

     2.  If so, are any amendments to past campaign statements
necessary in order to reflect when contributions were received by
the national association versus when the national association
transmitted the contributions to CAL-HY-PAC?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  Checks received which represent dues allocations from
members are contributions from the members, not from the national
association nor the California association.  In this case, both
associations act as an intermediary for the contributions and,
thus, must be listed as intermediaries on CAL-HY-PAC's campaign
statements.

     2.  CAL-HY-PAC must report all contributions received by the
committee or its agents during the period covered by the campaign
statement.  Therefore, if the national association received the
dues allocation during one campaign reporting period, and CAL-HY-
PAC reported receiving the contributions derived from those dues
allocations in a different reporting period, an amendment must be
filed to reflect the date that the national association, as the
committee's agent, received the funds.

ANALYSIS

     Since a portion of the members' dues collected by the
national association is "earmarked" for CAL-HY-PAC, the national
and California associations are acting as intermediaries for the
members' contributions.  (Section 84302.5; Regulation 18419.)
Since no one member contributes $100 or more in a calendar year,
the national and California associations must be itemized with
their addresses, the date of receipt of the contributions from the
California association, and the amount of the contributions
received on CAL-HY-PAC's campaign statement.  (Section 84302.)
For clarity, following the names and addresses of each
association, you should indicate that no contribution of $100 from
a single source was received.

     All contributions (pro-rated dues allocations) received by
the national association must be reported to CAL-HY-PAC's
treasurer no later than the closing date of any campaign statement
the committee is required to file.  (Section 84306.)  Therefore,
as an example, if the national association receives a dues
allocation from a member on June 15 of any given year, the amount

Robert Leidigh
Page Three

that will eventually be transmitted to CAL-HY-PAC must be reported
to the treasurer no later than June 30 of that same year, since
that is the close of the reporting period in which the
contribution was received.

If this reporting procedure has not been followed in the
past, you may file amendments to correct as many statements as
require amending. (Section 81004.)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG                FPPC

                                             Aug 21 12:26 PM '89


                    August 21, 1989

                    Kathryn E. Donovan
                    General Counsel
                    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
                    428 "J" Street, Suite 800
                    Sacramento, California 95814

                    RE:   REQUEST FOR FORMAL WRITTEN ADVICE

                    Dear Ms. Donovan:

                         I write on behalf of CAL-HY-PAC and its current
                    treasurer Lance H. Olson. I seek formal written advice
                    regarding the following facts.

                                          FACTS

                         1.   Lance H. Olson recently became treasurer for
                    CAL-HY-PAC.

                         2.   CAL-HY-PAC is a sponsored committee of the
                    California Dental Hygienists' Association within the
                    meaning of 2 California Code of Regulations Section
                    18419.

                         3.   CAL-HY-PAC is a broad based political
                    committee within the meaning of Government Code Section
                    85102(d) and 2 California Code of Regulations Section
LANCE H. OLSON          18502.
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEON Y. FONG                   4.   CAL-HY-PAC is funded through dues allocations
ROBERT E. LEIDEGH       from dues paid to the California Dental Hygienists'
                        Association by its members. The dues are paid annually
                        to the national association, which then forwards the
                        California association's pro rata share of the dues to
                        the California association. Of that pro rata share, a
                        certain amount is earmarked for contributions to CAL-
OF COUNSEL              HY-PAC.  In past years, the amount has been either $10
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member per member per year, or $5 per member per year.
California State Legislature
                             5.   In addition to the dues allocation, CAL-HY-PAC
                        has various other fundraising mechanisms. Principal
                        among these is a fundraiser held each year in
                        conjunction with its annual convention. Contributions
                        to that fundraiser are made directly by attending
                        association members. These contributions seldom exceed
                        $50 per member. Hence, few members of the association
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350   ever contribute $100 or more per year to the CAL-HY-PAC.
Sacramento, California 95814

TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

August 21, 1989
Page Two


     6.  Although the contributions are allocated in the dues
payments made by the members each annual period, because of
vagaries in the system, these payments arrive over several
months' time.  At least a few checks come in during any given
calendar month, but most come in during the winter and summer
months immediately surrounding the annual dues' dates.

     7.  In the past, the California Dental Hygienists'
Association has been sporadic in transmitting the allocated
portions earmarked for contributions to CAL-HY-PAC.  In fact, in
some instances, there has been more than a year delay in
transmitting the dues allocations.  This office is taking steps
to have the transmittal speeded up.

     8.  However, in our capacity as treasurer for CAL-HY-PAC, we
have recently received two checks which each exceed $2,500 from
the California Dental Hygienists' Association.  These checks
represent dues allocations for the following periods:

     July 1987; August 1987; October 1987; November 1987;
December 1987; January 1988; February 1988; April 1988; July
1988; August 1988; and September 1988.

     September 1987, March 1988, and more recent months have been
paid around the time of receipt of the funds from the national
association.  (It usually takes the national association a couple
months to pro-rate the dues from members and forward them to the
California association.)

     9.  Records exist which document who the members were at the
times the dues allocations were originally paid by the members
and received by the California association from the national
association.

     10.  CAL-HY-PAC has disclosed the contributions on its
campaign reports for the periods in which the funds are actually
received by CAL-HY-PAC.

QUESTIONS

     1.  May we treat the checks recently received as
contributions from the members of the association, pursuant to 2
California Code of Regulations Sections 18215 and 18419?

     2.  If so, are any amendments to past campaign statements
necessary in order to reflect this treatment?

August 21, 1989
Page Three


     3.  If not all of the funds, may a portion of the funds be
treated as contributions from the members; i.e., is there a limit
with respect to promptness of forwarding which we may apply as to
some of the funds?

     4.  If not, must we treat the checks as contributions from
the California Dental Hygienists' Association? (We have secured
a designation from the association that in this case the excess
amount is to be used for purposes other than making contributions
directly to candidates. We have placed these checks into a
separate segregated account pending receipt of your response to
this request.)

     5.  In the latter event, may the association designate
portions of the excess to be transferred from the separate,
segregated account into the CAL-HY-PAC candidate account in
future fiscal years in sums not to exceed $2,500?

     Very truly yours,

     OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

     [signature]
     ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

     cc:  CAL-HY-PAC
          California Dental Hygienists' Association

89-495

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG                                    FPPC

                                                        Aug 21  12:25 PM '89

August 21, 1989

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR FORMAL WRITTEN ADVICE

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     I write on behalf of CAL-HY-PAC and its current
treasurer Lance H. Olson. I seek formal written advice
regarding the following facts.

                                FACTS

     1. Lance H. Olson recently became treasurer for
CAL-HY-PAC.

     2. CAL-HY-PAC is a sponsored committee of the
California Dental Hygienists' Association within the
meaning of 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18419.

     3. CAL-HY-PAC is a broad based political
committee within the meaning of Government Code Section
85102(d) and 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18502.

     4. CAL-HY-PAC is funded through dues allocations
from dues paid to the California Dental Hygienists'
Association by its members. The dues are paid annually
to the national association, which then forwards the
California association's pro rata share of the dues to
the California association. Of that pro rata share, a
certain amount is earmarked for contributions to CAL-
HY-PAC. In past years, the amount has been either $10
per member per year, or $5 per member per year.

     5. In addition to the dues allocation, CAL-HY-PAC
has various other fundraising mechanisms. Principal
among these is a fundraiser held each year in
conjunction with its annual convention. Contributions
to that fundraiser are made directly by attending
association members. These contributions seldom exceed
$50 per member. Hence, few members of the association
ever contribute $100 or more per year to CAL-HY-PAC.

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

August 21, 1989
Page Two


     6.  Although the contributions are allocated in the dues
payments made by the members each annual period, because of
vagaries in the system, these payments arrive over several
months' time.  At least a few checks come in during any given
calendar month, but most come in during the winter and summer
months immediately surrounding the annual dues' dates.

     7.  In the past, the California Dental Hygienists'
Association has been sporadic in transmitting the allocated
portions earmarked for contributions to CAL-HY-PAC.  In fact, in
some instances, there has been more than a year delay in
transmitting the dues allocations.  This office is taking steps
to have the transmittal speed up.

     8.  However, in our capacity as treasurer for CAL-HY-PAC, we
have recently received two checks which each exceed $2,500 from
the California Dental Hygienists' Association.  These checks
represent dues allocations for the following periods:

     July 1987; August 1987; October 1987; November 1987;
December 1987; January 1988; February 1988; April 1988; July
1988; August 1988; and September 1988.

     September 1987, March 1988, and more recent months have been
paid around the time of receipt of the funds from the national
association. (It usually takes the national association a couple
months to pro-rate the dues from members and forward them to the
California association.)

     9.  Records exist which document who the members were at the
times the dues allocations were originally paid by the members
and received by the California association from the national
association.

     10.  CAL-HY-PAC has disclosed the contributions on its
campaign reports for the periods in which the funds are actually
received by CAL-HY-PAC.

QUESTIONS

     1.  May we treat the checks recently received as
contributions from the members of the association, pursuant to 2
California Code of Regulations Sections 18215 and 18419?

     2.  If so, are any amendments to past campaign statements
necessary in order to reflect this treatment?

August 21, 1989
Page Three


     3.  If not all of the funds, may a portion of the funds be
treated as contributions from the members; i.e., is there a limit
with respect to promptness of forwarding which we may apply to
some of the funds?

     4.  If not, must we treat the checks as contributions from
the California Dental Hygienists' Association?  (We have secured
a designation from the association that in this case the excess
amount is to be used for purposes other than making contributions
directly to candidates.  We have placed these checks into a
separate segregated account pending receipt of your response to
this request.)

     5.  In the latter event, may the association designate
portions of the excess to be transferred from the separate,
segregated account into the CAL-HY-PAC candidate account in
future fiscal years in sums not to exceed $2,500?

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

[signature]

ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

cc:  CAL-HY-PAC
     California Dental Hygienists' Association

August 21, 1989


Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-495

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on August 21, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

QUESTION: 1. 
May checks recently received by CAL-HY-PAC be treated as 
contributions from the members of the association? 
2. 
If so, are any amendments to past campaign statements 
necessary in order to reflect when contributions were received by 
the national association versus when the national association 
transmitted the contributions to CAL-HY-PAC?

CONCLUSION: 1. 
Checks received which represent dues allocations from 
members are contributions from the members, not from the national 
association nor the California association. 
In this case, both 
associations act as an intermediary for the contributions and, 
thus, must be listed as intermediaries on CAL-HY-PAC's campaign 
statements. 
2. 
CAL-HY-PAC must report all contributions received by the 
committee or its agents during the period covered by the campaign 
statement. 
Therefore, if the national association received the 
dues allocation during one campaign reporting period, and CAL-HY-
PAC reported receiving the contributions derived from those dues 
allocations in a different reporting period, an amendment must be 
filed to reflect the date that the national association, as the 
committee's agent, received the funds.
